phase: P2-15 offline preview ledger / archive index

oss_recon_gate.reference: OSV vulnerability feed metadata (stable schema_version literal + Z-suffixed UTC timestamps + stable IDs)
oss_recon_gate.reason: OSV's pattern of a required const schema_version string plus closed, typed fields and Z-suffixed UTC timestamps is the cleanest fit for a small, stable index contract and aligns 1:1 with how preview_manifest/1.0 already encodes its version and time fields.

oss_recon_gate.reference: SARIF runs[] and result artifact references (versioned tool/run + array of bound records)
oss_recon_gate.reason: Borrow only the shape: a versioned producer block plus an array of records that each bind a specific run by id and reference an external artifact. Do not adopt SARIF's result/finding lifecycle, taxonomies, locations, fixes, or any importer-style semantics; preview_ledger/1.0 must remain a binding index, not a findings format or importer surface.

oss_recon_gate.reference: CycloneDX/BOM-style component index metadata (per-component {alg, content, name} hashes)
oss_recon_gate.reason: The per-entry {algorithm, content_hash, relative_path, size_bytes} descriptor maps cleanly to binding each persisted preview_manifest.json file by digest. Do not adopt full BOM scope, supplier/license fields, vulnerability sub-objects, or any external resolution/fetch behavior.

oss_recon_gate.reference: DefectDojo importer/test metadata (engagement/test lifecycle, upload state)
oss_recon_gate.reason: Tightly coupled to a service, engagement/test lifecycle, finding ingestion, and remote tracking state. Adopting any of these fields would imply upload, triage, or remote sync surfaces, which violate the offline/data-only boundary.

oss_recon_gate.reference: Nuclei output/template metadata for scan-result indexing
oss_recon_gate.reason: Conventions are driven by live scanning output, template-source assumptions, and severity heuristics. Embedding them risks normalizing target-touching defaults and confusing the ledger (a manifest-of-manifests index) with a scan-result aggregator.

approved_scope: Introduce a closed, versioned JSON Schema at modules/_schema/preview_ledger.schema.json defining preview_ledger/1.0 as a manifest-of-manifests index.

Introduce a standalone, read-only, stdlib-only validator at scripts/validate_preview_ledger.py that validates a ledger JSON document against the schema and against the preview_manifest.json files it references by recomputing SHA-256 and verifying paths.
Bind each ledger entry strictly to preview_manifest/1.0 by requiring schema_version_observed == "preview_manifest/1.0" per entry; ledger is not a generic file index.

Reuse the P2-14 fixture tree by referencing tests/fixtures/preview_manifest/1.0/valid_minimal/ as the golden manifest target for a committed valid ledger fixture under tests/fixtures/preview_ledger/1.0/valid_minimal/.
Add a focused test suite at scripts/test_preview_ledger.py covering valid fixtures and per-rule invalid cases (see test_requirements).

Document the contract addition in modules/_schema/README.md with the same strict-equal versioning rule used for preview_manifest/1.0 (no silent 1.0 drift; additive fields require a future minor version).
Validator CLI output is single-line JSON with its own schema_version literal preview_ledger_validation/1.0 so future tooling consumes it without re-parsing prose.
out_of_scope: Builder/indexer (scripts/build_preview_ledger.py). Defer to a separate phase (P2-16) with its own review; that phase must justify the new write surface, the filesystem scan, and the opt-in flag explicitly.
Any automatic scan of runs/*/preview/preview_manifest.json from CI, hooks, module_runner, or pre-commit.
Wiring module_runner or any executor to emit, update, append to, or rely on the ledger.
Agent-assisted triage logic, archive promotion, retention/aging policy, deletion or move of bundles.
Multi-version support: ledger must reject preview_manifest/1.1+, preview_manifest/2.0, or any non-literal preview_manifest/1.0 entries.
Per-entry findings, evidence, severity, status_tags, target identifiers, host names, secrets, environment values, or notes that imply remote tracking state.
Network access, subprocess, threads, or any third-party schema library.
Writing, repairing, renaming, deleting, or moving any preview bundle file or ledger file from the validator. Validator is pure-read.
Any field that implies upload, publish, sync_status, remote_id, dojo_engagement, triage_state, or external endpoint.

Re-running the preview_manifest validator's full artifact-integrity sweep from inside the ledger validator (it must only hash the preview_manifest.json file itself; inner-artifact integrity remains the preview_manifest validator's responsibility, invoked separately when desired).
required_contract_fields: ledger_id (string, matches the same safe-id regex used by run_id: ^[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9._-]{2,127}$)
producer.name (string, const: "preview_ledger" — a curated/declarative producer name, not a live tool; reserved for an eventual P2-16 builder)
entries (array, closed; items as defined below; minItems may be 0 to allow an empty curated ledger)
entries[].run_id (string, same safe-run-id regex as preview_manifest/1.0)
entries[].preview_manifest_relative_path (string, must match exactly runs/<run_id>/preview/preview_manifest.json with no '..', no leading '/', no Windows drive, no backslash, no '//', no '/./')
entries[].observed_at_utc (string, Z-suffixed UTC; the time the ledger entry was recorded; must be <= ledger.created_at_utc and not in the future)
entries[].notes (string, optional, max 256 chars, must match a redaction-safe regex with no path separators / URLs / @ / host indicators)
additionalProperties: false enforced at the top-level object, at producer, and at each entry item (closed schema everywhere).
validator_requirements: Standard-library only (json, re, hashlib, pathlib, datetime, argparse, dataclasses, sys, typing). No third-party schema libs. No socket, urllib, http, subprocess, threading, or multiprocessing imports.

Pure read-only over the ledger file and the referenced preview_manifest.json files. The validator never creates, writes, repairs, renames, deletes, executes modules, opens network clients, or touches targets.
Strict JSON parse for the ledger document: reject duplicate keys via an object_pairs_hook; reject trailing data after the JSON document; reject non-UTF-8 with a typed error.

Schema-shape validation: enforce required fields, reject unknown fields at every object level, enforce all const literals, enforce all regex patterns, enforce integer non-negativity and string-vs-integer types strictly (booleans must not satisfy integer types).

Strict-equal version negotiation: ledger.schema_version must be the exact literal preview_ledger/1.0; each entries[].schema_version_observed must be the exact literal preview_manifest/1.0. Any drift is a hard reject.
Duplicate-run guard: each entries[].run_id must appear at most once; second occurrence is rejected with a typed error and the offending entry index.

Path safety: each entries[].preview_manifest_relative_path must equal PurePosixPath('runs') / run_id / 'preview' / 'preview_manifest.json' exactly. Reject absolute paths, Windows drive prefixes, backslashes, leading './' or '/', '..' anywhere, '//', '/./', or trailing dot segments.

Symlink rejection: reject if the ledger file itself, any parent of the ledger file, the resolved preview_manifest.json path, or any parent of that path is a symlink. OSError during the symlink check is treated as denial (fail-closed).

File binding: for each entry, read the referenced preview_manifest.json by bytes only, recompute SHA-256, and compare to entries[].preview_manifest_sha256. Compare file length to entries[].preview_manifest_size_bytes. Both must match.

Do not parse, walk, or hash the four inner artifacts (run.json, module_inputs.json, module_results.json, bundle_consistency.json). Inner-artifact integrity remains the preview_manifest validator's responsibility; the ledger only binds the preview_manifest.json file.
Timestamp checks: ledger.created_at_utc must not be in the future; each entries[].observed_at_utc must not be in the future and must not be later than ledger.created_at_utc.
entry_count equality: ledger.entry_count must equal len(entries); mismatch is rejected.

Path resolution base: the validator computes the manifest path relative to a caller-supplied repo root (CLI: positional ledger_file plus --repo-root REPO_ROOT, defaulting to the ledger file's parent if and only if that parent matches a runs/ container heuristic; otherwise --repo-root is required). The repo-root is normalized via Path.resolve() once, and resolved manifest paths must remain inside repo_root/runs/.
Fail-closed: PreviewLedgerValidation defaults verdict='deny'; flips to 'allow' only when the errors list is empty.

Output: a single-line JSON document with schema_version preview_ledger_validation/1.0, fields {validated_schema_version, verdict, errors[{code,path,message,expected?,observed?}], error_codes, warnings}. Errors must be redaction-safe: do not echo raw file contents, target identifiers, or environment values; use _type_name()-style placeholders for 'observed'.
Exit code: 0 on allow, nonzero on deny. The validator is not wired into module_runner or any other write-path.
test_requirements: Golden valid fixture: a ledger at tests/fixtures/preview_ledger/1.0/valid_minimal/preview_ledger.json with one entry referencing the committed tests/fixtures/preview_manifest/1.0/valid_minimal/preview_manifest.json bundle passes validation.
Empty-entries valid case: a ledger with entries=[] and entry_count=0 passes (the contract permits a curated empty ledger).
Each required top-level field missing -> rejection with field path (schema_version, ledger_id, created_at_utc, producer, producer.name, producer.version, entry_count, entries).
additionalProperties: unknown top-level field -> rejection; unknown field inside producer -> rejection; unknown field inside an entries[] item -> rejection.
schema_version drift -> rejection (preview_ledger/1.0.0, preview_ledger/1.1, preview_ledger/2.0, missing, non-string).
Per-entry schema_version_observed drift -> rejection (preview_manifest/1.1, preview_manifest/2.0, missing, non-string).
Unsafe ledger_id and unsafe entries[].run_id (path chars, spaces, traversal, empty, leading dot) -> rejection.
entries[].preview_manifest_relative_path with '..', leading '/', backslash, drive letter, '//', '/./', or not equal to runs/<run_id>/preview/preview_manifest.json -> rejection.
Duplicate run_id across entries -> rejection citing both entry indices.
Hash mismatch between entries[].preview_manifest_sha256 and the actual preview_manifest.json contents -> rejection.
Size mismatch between entries[].preview_manifest_size_bytes and the actual file length -> rejection.
Missing preview_manifest.json file (path-correct entry but no file on disk) -> rejection.
Symlinked preview_manifest.json, symlinked preview/ directory, symlinked run/ directory, and symlinked ledger file -> each rejected.

Timestamp checks: ledger.created_at_utc in the future -> rejection; entries[].observed_at_utc in the future -> rejection; entries[].observed_at_utc > ledger.created_at_utc -> rejection; non-Z-suffixed or non-RFC3339-shaped timestamp -> rejection.
entry_count != len(entries) -> rejection.
Strict JSON parse: duplicate JSON keys -> rejection; trailing data after the document -> rejection; non-UTF-8 ledger bytes -> rejection.
notes field: over-length notes -> rejection; notes containing '/', '\\', '@', 'http://', 'https://', or an IP-like or host-like token -> rejection (redaction-safe vocabulary).
Boolean-vs-integer: entry_count or preview_manifest_size_bytes set to true/false -> rejection (booleans must not satisfy integer types).

Pure-function guarantee: validator run leaves filesystem mtimes, sizes, and contents of the ledger file, the referenced preview_manifest.json, and the bundle artifacts unchanged (snapshot before/after).
Static import test: scripts/validate_preview_ledger.py imports do not include socket, urllib, http, subprocess, threading, or multiprocessing.
CLI behavior: nonzero exit on any deny verdict; single-line JSON output; no stderr noise on success; --json flag accepted for compatibility with the preview_manifest validator convention.
safety_boundary: No scans, probes, callbacks, exploit/fuzz/brute-force, or target-touching behavior introduced.
No module execution, no subprocess, no network, no scanner adapters, no template fetching, no threads.
No changes to config/scope.txt, program scope, safe_target, dry-run gates, or live-mode toggles.
No findings, evidence, reports, notifications, publishing surfaces, or remote-tracking fields added.
No loot/secret access; validator errors are redaction-safe and use _type_name()-style placeholders for observed values.
Validator is read-only: it never writes, repairs, deletes, moves, or renames the ledger file, the preview_manifest.json file, or any bundle artifact.
Ledger is a binding index of preview_manifest/1.0 records, not a generic file index. Each entry is constrained to runs/<run_id>/preview/preview_manifest.json with an exact path equality check.
No automatic filesystem scan in P2-15. Building a ledger from filesystem state is deferred to P2-16 and must justify its write surface and opt-in semantics separately.
No wiring of the ledger or its validator into module_runner, discovery, the policy gate, or any commit/PR hook in P2-15.
Strict-equal version negotiation: preview_ledger/1.0 and per-entry preview_manifest/1.0 are exact-literal; any drift is a hard reject. No silent multi-version support.

Closed schema (additionalProperties: false at every object) plus an exact-path constraint per entry plus a duplicate-run-id guard prevents the ledger from being repurposed as a generic write/output mechanism or a covert publishing channel.
Stricter rule wins: if this contract conflicts with .hermes.md, scope, or the OSS Recon Gate, defer to the stricter constraint.
non_blocking_recommendations: Defer the builder/indexer (scripts/build_preview_ledger.py) to P2-16 and gate it behind an explicit --build / --opt-in flag; document the new write surface, the runs/ scan boundary, and the symlink/path-traversal posture in that phase's direction doc before implementation.

Co-locate ledger fixtures under tests/fixtures/preview_ledger/1.0/{valid_minimal, invalid_*}/ and reuse tests/fixtures/preview_manifest/1.0/valid_minimal/ as the referenced bundle so the two contracts share a single source of truth for the golden case.

Add a one-paragraph compatibility rule to modules/_schema/README.md mirroring the preview_manifest/1.0 rule: preview_ledger/1.0 is strict-equal; additive optional fields require a new minor version and a new schema file; the validator never silently upgrades or downgrades.

Reserve (but do not implement in P2-15) a future entries[].preview_manifest_validation_status field that would record an allow/deny verdict from the preview_manifest validator. Deferring it keeps 1.0 minimal and avoids embedding stale validation state in a committed file.
Reserve (but do not implement in P2-15) a top-level ledger_digest field plan for a later phase that wants tamper-evident sealing of the ledger itself. Document the deferral rather than adding it now.

Keep the validator CLI output a single-line JSON document (matching the preview_manifest validator convention) so a future agent-assisted triage consumer can pipe and diff results without re-parsing prose.
Add a contracts/CHANGELOG.md-style entry policy (or extend the existing README rule) so any future preview_ledger/1.x bump is reviewed as its own T3 change with its own direction doc and fixtures.

When the builder lands in P2-16, require it to call the preview_manifest validator on each candidate bundle before writing the entry, and to refuse to emit entries for bundles that do not pass; this keeps the ledger's allow-listed records bound to validator-approved bundles without making the ledger validator itself re-run that sweep.
